symptoms.symptom_name: Sudden, severe, tearing, maximal-at-onset chest pain radiating to the interscapular back

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Tearing, maximal-at-onset chest pain radiating to the back is highly specific for aortic dissection.
A tearing quality radiating to the back is distinct from the crushing retrosternal pain of MI - important because aspirin is harmful in dissection.

symptoms.symptom_name: Known Marfan syndrome with dilated aortic root (beta-blocker non-adherence)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Marfan with aortic root dilatation is a major predisposing condition for dissection.
Pain unaffected by movement/breathing argues against a musculoskeletal or pleuritic cause.

symptoms.symptom_name: Profound lightheadedness and nausea (possible early haemodynamic compromise)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Lightheadedness/nausea may reflect early haemodynamic compromise (blood loss into the false lumen, tamponade, or aortic regurgitation).

symptoms.symptom_name: Marfanoid features (tall, long limbs, arachnodactyly, ectopia lentis)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Marfanoid habitus with ectopia lentis supports the connective-tissue disorder predisposing to dissection.

symptoms.symptom_name: Inter-arm BP differential (>20 mmHg), diminished left radial pulse, and a new aortic regurgitation murmur

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A BP differential/pulse deficit with a new aortic regurgitation murmur is a classic sign of ascending aortic dissection.

symptoms.symptom_name: Aortic root dilatation with a dissection flap on CT aortography

investigation_details.expected_result: Dilated aortic root with an intimal dissection flap and false lumen, defining the type/extent (probable Type A)
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: The dissection is unconfirmed and life-saving emergency surgery cannot be planned.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A dissection flap on CT aortography confirms the diagnosis and guides emergency surgery.

symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Acute aortic dissection (Type A) in Marfan syndrome
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Sudden tearing maximal-at-onset chest-to-back pain in known Marfan with a dilated aortic root, unrelenting and non-pleuritic with early haemodynamic symptoms, is a supreme surgical emergency requiring immediate ambulance transfer, stillness, and explicitly NO aspirin.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms the dissection and its type/extent to plan emergency surgery; done urgently in hospital.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Emergency cardiothoracic surgical assessment with BP/HR control
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Type A dissection is a surgical emergency; blood-pressure and heart-rate control reduce shear stress pending surgery.
Contraindicated in suspected aortic dissection - antiplatelet/anticoagulant therapy worsens bleeding from the tear.
